name: file_ops
description: Manage and explore project files using one consistent shape: each operation has only `operation` and `args`. Use forward-slash Godot paths (`res://...` preferred) for Windows, macOS, and Linux compatibility. Read-only list/glob/rg operations may inspect `res://...` project files and any `user://...` path in the current project's Godot user data folder, including logs, runtime artifacts, screenshots, saves, cache files, and saved result JSON. Write operations (copy, move, delete, create_dir) stay project-scoped; do not use them for `user://...` artifacts. Supports list, glob, rg, copy, move, delete, and create_dir. Read-only operations can be batched and may run in parallel; write operations always run sequentially. `rg` uses bundled ripgrep directly with an argument array, not a shell. The wrapper automatically adds safe output flags: `--no-messages`, `--with-filename`, `--no-heading`, `--path-separator /`, and metadata exclusion globs for `*.uid`, `*.import`, and `*.remap`. Hard limit: include at most 5 operations per call.

parallel.description: If true, contiguous read-only operations may execute in parallel while preserving result order. Write operations remain sequential. Default true.

operations.description: Array of file operations. Every item must use only `operation` and `args`. Positional contracts: list [path?, flags...]; glob [path, pattern, flags...]; rg [ripgrep flags..., pattern, paths...]; copy [source, destination, flags...]; move [source, destination, flags...]; delete [path, flags...]; create_dir [path].

operation.description: Operation name only. Never combine operation and path. Valid values: list, glob, rg, copy, move, delete, create_dir.

args.description: String tokens for the operation. Do not pass a shell command string. Put each flag/path/pattern in its own array item. Use forward slashes and `res://` paths where possible. For read-only list/glob/rg, any `user://...` path in the current project's Godot user data folder is allowed; use returned log, screenshot, runtime artifact, save, cache, and result JSON paths directly. Common flags: list --hidden --unimportant --files-only --dirs-only; glob --hidden --unimportant --dirs --max-results N; rg -n -S -e PATTERN --glob GLOB; copy --recursive --overwrite; move --overwrite; delete --recursive. For `rg`, do not pass `--max-columns` or `-M`.
